purpose: Extend the existing Eclipse plugin adapter with a small set of useful Eclipse-native views while keeping Ananta as the control plane.
goal: The Eclipse plugin should manage Ananta work through a few clear Eclipse views for goals, tasks, artifacts, context, review and blueprint/work-profile usage without turning into a second standalone frontend.
success_criteria: The plugin offers a compact set of Eclipse-native views that map to real Ananta workflows.
Views remain thin and reuse existing Ananta APIs and read models instead of duplicating orchestration logic.
Users can manage daily IDE work without constantly switching to the browser.
The plugin still stays maintainable and does not become a full second web UI inside Eclipse.

tasks.title: View strategy for Eclipse plugin define
acceptance_criteria: A short design note defines which views belong to the Eclipse plugin MVP, which belong to phase 2 and which stay browser-only.
The note states that Eclipse views are work-oriented and do not replace the full Ananta web UI.
Each planned view is mapped to concrete Ananta backend flows.

tasks.title: Goal and quick action view implement
acceptance_criteria: A dedicated Eclipse view allows entering a goal or selecting a quick action.
The view supports official actions such as analyze, review, patch, new-project and evolve-project.
The view can include bounded editor/project context before submission.

tasks.title: Task list view implement
acceptance_criteria: A dedicated Eclipse view lists current Ananta tasks.
The view shows status, title, review-required state and next step summary.
Users can open a selected task for more detail without leaving Eclipse.

tasks.title: Artifact view implement
acceptance_criteria: A dedicated Eclipse view lists returned artifacts such as analysis summaries, review outputs, research artifacts or proposals.
Artifacts are readable inside Eclipse in a bounded form.
Users can jump from artifact entries to related tasks/goals or open the full object in the browser.

tasks.title: Context inspection view implement
acceptance_criteria: A dedicated view shows which editor selection, file path, project context or extra files will be sent to Ananta.
Users can remove or adjust context before submission.
The view makes context packaging transparent and bounded.

tasks.title: Basic task detail view implement
acceptance_criteria: Users can inspect a selected task in a richer detail view.
The detail view shows summary, pipeline/routing hints, artifacts and latest status.
The detail view is readable without becoming a full ops dashboard.

tasks.title: Review and proposal view implement
acceptance_criteria: A dedicated view can display change review results, findings and proposals.
Where applicable, the view supports explicit approve/reject actions under existing policy constraints.
The view keeps review work visible and auditable.

tasks.title: Blueprint and work-profile view implement
acceptance_criteria: A dedicated view exposes standard blueprints and their work-profile summaries.
Users can see what a blueprint is for, which goal modes it recommends and what outputs it tends to produce.
The view helps users choose a suitable starting path from inside Eclipse.

tasks.title: Connection and runtime status view implement
acceptance_criteria: An optional view shows connection status, selected Ananta endpoint and basic runtime health.
The view can surface whether the backend is reachable and whether required capabilities are available.
The view remains lightweight and does not duplicate the full admin settings surface.

tasks.title: View navigation and linking model define
acceptance_criteria: A small navigation model defines how goal, task, artifact and review views link to one another.
The user can move between core views without losing task context.
Cross-links to the Ananta web UI remain available for deeper inspection.

tasks.title: Unified selection synchronization across views add
acceptance_criteria: Selecting a task or artifact in one view can update related detail panes or linked views.
The synchronization stays predictable and not overly magical.
Users can always tell which object is currently active.

tasks.title: Minimal view state persistence add
acceptance_criteria: Core view state such as selected connection profile or last opened filters is restored reasonably across IDE restarts.
Sensitive state is not persisted unsafely.
Persistence reduces friction without adding hidden complexity.

tasks.title: Task filters and grouping add
acceptance_criteria: Task view supports useful filters such as active, done, blocked or review-required.
Grouping by goal or status is possible where helpful.
The feature improves usability without becoming a full project-management clone.

tasks.title: Artifact rendering modes define
acceptance_criteria: Artifact view supports a small number of rendering modes such as summary, markdown-like text and proposal/review output.
The plugin can render common artifact types meaningfully.
Unsupported types degrade cleanly to raw text or open-in-browser.

tasks.title: Review and diff rendering improve for Eclipse
acceptance_criteria: Change-oriented artifacts can be shown in a form suitable for IDE review work.
File references are clickable where possible.
The plugin does not silently apply changes just because they are visible.

tasks.title: Context source badges and provenance hints add
acceptance_criteria: Views can show whether context or outputs came from selection, repo context, task memory, research or other sources when applicable.
This remains lightweight and readable.
The feature improves trust in what the plugin is showing.

tasks.title: First-run perspective/layout define
acceptance_criteria: A recommended Eclipse layout or perspective for Ananta views is documented or shipped.
The first-run layout favors Goal, Task, Artifact and Context views.
Users do not need to manually assemble a sensible working layout.

tasks.title: Browser fallback policy for complex views define
acceptance_criteria: A clear rule defines when Eclipse should show data directly and when it should send users to the Ananta web UI.
Complex admin or deep audit screens remain browser-first unless there is strong IDE value.
This policy prevents the plugin from becoming a second full frontend.

tasks.title: View-specific error and empty-state UX add
acceptance_criteria: Each core view has helpful empty and error states.
Users can distinguish no data, disconnected backend and permission issues.
The plugin stays understandable under degraded conditions.

tasks.title: Accessibility and keyboard usage pass for views add
acceptance_criteria: Core actions in major views can be reached without excessive mouse dependency.
Basic keyboard navigation across list/detail interactions works.
The initial view set remains usable in everyday development flow.

tasks.title: Manual smoke checklist for multi-view workflow add
acceptance_criteria: A manual checklist covers realistic flow across Goal, Task, Context and Artifact views.
The checklist is short enough for release smoke testing.
The checklist catches broken linking and stale state between views.

tasks.title: Automated tests for view models and view coordination add
acceptance_criteria: Core view-model logic is covered by tests.
Tests protect synchronization between selected task, artifact and detail views.
Tests reduce regressions as more views are added.

tasks.title: Knowledge and sources view evaluate for later phase
acceptance_criteria: A later-phase evaluation explores whether repo context, task memory and future wiki/research sources deserve their own Eclipse view.
The result stays separate from the first useful plugin release.
The MVP is not blocked by this richer knowledge visualization.

tasks.title: Future advanced admin views isolate
acceptance_criteria: Possible advanced views such as provider health, governance detail or deeper audit inspection are documented separately.
These ideas do not bloat the main Eclipse plugin roadmap.
The plugin keeps a clear difference between work views and operator/admin views.
